title.emphasis: Part III: The "Client" Relationship and Fiduciary Duties

section.para: The most stringent standard of care recognized by California law is a fiduciary duty. This duty arises in relationships where one party (the "client" or beneficiary) places special trust and confidence in another (the fiduciary). While often associated with professionals like attorneys and trustees, the duty can be imposed in any relationship where such confidence is reposed and accepted.5
A fiduciary relationship imposes several fundamental duties that are non-disclaimable as a matter of public policy 5:

listitem.para: Duty of Loyalty: This is the paramount duty, requiring the fiduciary to act solely in the best interest of the client. The fiduciary must avoid any conflicts of interest or acts of self-dealing.6
Duty of Care: A fiduciary must manage the client's affairs with the reasonable care, skill, and caution that a prudent person would exercise in a similar situation.6

Duty of Confidentiality: This duty requires the fiduciary to preserve the client's secrets "at every peril to himself or herself".6 It is a far more rigorous standard than in a typical commercial relationship.

title.emphasis: 3.2 Application to Digital Platforms and Services

section.para: The concept of a fiduciary duty is increasingly being applied to the digital realm. A platform that acts as a "custodian" of a user's digital assets may be seen as taking on fiduciary-like responsibilities. California's Revised Uniform Fiduciary Access to Digital Assets Act defines a "user" as a person with an account with a "custodian," and imposes duties of care, loyalty, and confidentiality on fiduciaries charged with managing such assets.18

An argument can be made that when a platform encourages users to entrust it with vast amounts of personal data, creative content, and other digital assets, and then uses those assets for its own commercial benefit, it has voluntarily accepted a position of trust and confidence. In such a scenario, a court could find that the platform owes its users fiduciary duties. An explicit disclaimer of these duties in the Terms of Service would be highly vulnerable to being struck down as unconscionable, as it would represent an attempt to waive the very essence of the trust-based relationship the platform has cultivated.
